---
title: "The tractoverse"
subtitle: "White matter tractography analysis in R"
author: 
  - name: "Aymeric Stamm"
    affiliation: Department of Mathematics Jean Leray, UMR CNRS 6629, Nantes, France
date: "2026-07-09"
date-format: long
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    logo: Banner-squares.png
    footer: "Aymeric Stamm &nbsp;·&nbsp; [astamm.github.io](https://astamm.github.io) &nbsp;·&nbsp; aymeric.stamm@cnrs.fr &nbsp;·&nbsp; useR! 2026 &nbsp;&nbsp; ![](Banner-squares.png){height=22px style='vertical-align:middle;margin-left:6px;'}"
    slide-number: c/t
    progress: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    code-line-numbers: false
    highlight-style: dracula
    fig-align: center
    css: custom.css
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#0d1117"
brand: false
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## The brain's white matter

::: {.cols-2}

:::: {.callout style="font-size:0.8em;"}
**Diffusion MRI** probes water motion in tissue at the micron scale.

- Axons restrict diffusion **along** their axis → **anisotropy**
- Magnetic field gradient attenuates the MR signal → diffusion-weighted images
- Voxel-level diffusion **model** → local fiber orientation and tissue properties

**Tractography** follows these orientations across voxels to reconstruct 3-D fiber trajectories which carry microstructural information.

![Taken from Wikimedia Commons - under [CC BY 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/deed.en) license](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d1/Comparison_between_anatomic_dissections_using_the_fiber_dissection_technique_and_tractography_PMC3640224_figure_F4.jpg){width=60% fig-align="center"}
::::

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
%%| eval: true
%%{init:{"theme":"dark","themeVariables":{"fontSize":"16px"}}}%%
flowchart TD
  A["🧠 Diffusion MRI<br/>(raw signal)"]:::blue --> B["Preprocessing<br/>(Eddy, motion, B0)"]:::grey
  B --> C["Diffusion model<br/>(DTI, CSD, NODDI…)"]:::blue
  C --> D["Tractography algorithm<br/>(deterministic / probabilistic)"]:::blue
  D --> E["🔗 Streamlines<br/>(ordered 3-D points)"]:::green
  E --> F1["Tractometry<br/>(microstructure along tract)"]:::teal
  E --> F2["Connectomics<br/>(region-to-region graph)"]:::teal
  E --> F3["Shape analysis<br/>(FDA, optimal transport)"]:::teal

  classDef blue fill:#1c3d5a,stroke:#58a6ff,color:#e6edf3
  classDef green fill:#1a3a2a,stroke:#3fb950,color:#e6edf3
  classDef teal fill:#1a3035,stroke:#39c5cf,color:#e6edf3
  classDef grey fill:#21262d,stroke:#8b949e,color:#8b949e
```

:::

---

## The R gap

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Major tractography tools** — none in R:

| Software | Language | Analysis? |
|:---------|:---------|:---------:|
| MRtrix3 | C++ | Connectomics |
| DIPY | Python | Connectomics + Tractometry |
| pyAFQ | Python | Tractometry |
| Scilpy | Python | Connectomics + Tractometry |
| DSI Studio | C++ | Connectomics |

*(15+ tools surveyed — **0 in R**)*
::::

:::

::: {.column width="10%"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**R packages on CRAN** for diffusion MRI:

<div class="callout-box warn">
<code>dti</code> — diffusion tensor fitting only, GPL, **47** dependencies
</div>
<div class="callout-box warn">
<code>tractor.base</code> — preprocessing pipeline, no streamline analysis
</div>
<div class="callout-box">
<code>dmri.tracking</code> — <strong>archived ✗</strong>
</div>

No R package provides comprehensively:

<span class="chip">streamline objects</span>
<span class="chip">tractometry</span>
<span class="chip">connectomics</span>
<span class="chip">shape analysis</span>
<span class="chip">3-D visualization</span>
<span class="chip">microstructure simulation</span>
::::

:::

:::

---

## The tractoverse

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
A **modular** collection of R packages for tractography analysis — inspired by the tidyverse.
::::

::: {.cols-2}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
| Package | CRAN | Role |
|:--------|:----:|:-----|
| <span class="pkg-badge cran">fiber</span> | ✅ | Data structures |
| <span class="pkg-badge pend">riot</span> | ⏳ | I/O (awaiting rvtk) |
| <span class="pkg-badge gh">rtists</span> | 🔜 | 3-D visualization |
| <span class="pkg-badge gh">mascot</span> | 🔜 | Reference datasets |
| <span class="pkg-badge cran">midi</span> | ✅ | Microstructure sim |
| <span class="pkg-badge gh">bot</span> | 🔜 | Optimal transport |

<br>
<span class="dim">+ <code>rvtk</code> — VTK infrastructure for R (v0.2.0 submitted)</span>
::::

:::: {style="font-size:0.8em;"}

:::: {.timeline}
<div class="timeline-item cran"><span class="tl-date">Apr 2024</span><span class="tl-label">midi 0.1.0</span><span class="tl-desc">first CRAN release</span></div>
<div class="timeline-item pend"><span class="tl-date">May 2026</span><span class="tl-label">rvtk 0.1.3</span><span class="tl-desc">first CRAN release</span></div>
<div class="timeline-item cran"><span class="tl-date">Jun 2026</span><span class="tl-label">fiber 0.1.0</span><span class="tl-desc">first CRAN release</span></div>
<div class="timeline-item cran"><span class="tl-date">Jul 2026</span><span class="tl-label">fiber 0.2.0<br/>midi 0.2.0</span><span class="tl-desc">latest updates</span></div>
<div class="timeline-item pend"><span class="tl-date">Jul 2026</span><span class="tl-label">rvtk 0.2.0</span><span class="tl-desc">under submission</span></div>
<div class="timeline-item pend"><span class="tl-date">soon™</span><span class="tl-label">riot<br/>rtists<br/>mascot<br/>bot</span><span class="tl-desc">pending / in development</span></div>
::::

::::

:::

---

## `fiber` — S7 data structures {.section-header}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**The backbone** of the tractoverse. Like `tibble` is to the tidyverse. Three [S7](https://rconsortium.github.io/S7/) classes:
::::

::: {.cols-2}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}

<div class="callout-box ok">
<strong>streamline</strong> — one tract<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@points</code> ($P \times 3$ matrix)<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@point_data</code> (list of length-$P$ numeric vectors)<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@streamline_data</code> (list of scalars)
</div>

<div class="callout-box ok">
<strong>bundle</strong> — collection of streamlines<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@streamlines</code> (list of $S$ streamlines)<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@streamline_data</code> (list of length-$S$ numeric vectors)<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@bundle_data</code> (list of scalars)
</div>

<div class="callout-box ok">
<strong>bundle_set</strong> — multi-subject / multi-session<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@bundles</code> (list of $B$ bundles)<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@bundle_data</code> (list of length-$B$ numeric vectors)<br>
<code style="color:#3fb950">@set_data</code> (list of scalars)
</div>

::::

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}

<div class="callout-box warn">
<strong>Data lifting and subsetting</strong><br>
✅ <code style="color:#3fb950">@streamline_data</code> common to all streamlines in a bundle → **lifted** to <code style="color:#3fb950">@streamline_data</code> of the bundle;<br>
✅ <code style="color:#3fb950">@bundle_data</code> common to all bundles in a set → **lifted** to <code style="color:#3fb950">@set_data</code> of the bundle set.<br>
✅ Upon subsetting, lifted data is **propagated down** to the lower-level objects.
</div>

::::

:::

---

## `fiber` — quick start

```r
library(fiber)

t <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 50)

# Build a helix streamline
sl <- streamline(
  points     = cbind(X = cos(t), Y = sin(t), Z = t / (2 * pi)),
  point_data = list(FA = runif(50, 0.3, 0.9))
)
sl
#> ── Object of class `fiber::streamline()` with 50 points. ──
#>  • Point attributes: FA
#>  • Streamline attributes: none

# Geometry on streamlines
get_curvilinear_length(sl)   #> [1] 6.36
get_sinuosity(sl)            #> [1] 6.36

# Build a bundle and reparametrize
b <- bundle(lapply(1:10, \(i) reparametrize(sl, n_points = 20L)))
b
#> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle()` with 10 streamlines [20-20 pts/sl] ──

# Multi-subject bundle set
bs <- bundle_set(list("sub-01" = b, "sub-02" = b))
bs
#> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle_set()` with 2 bundles [10-10 sl/bundle] ──
```

::: {.callout-box .ok style="margin-top:1rem; font-size:0.7em;"}
✅ On CRAN &nbsp;|&nbsp;
📦 Install: `install.packages("fiber")` &nbsp;|&nbsp;
🔗 [tractoverse.github.io/fiber](https://tractoverse.github.io/fiber/)
:::

---

## `riot` — R I/O for Tractography {.section-header}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Read & write** tractography files in R — like `readr` is to the tidyverse.
::::

:::{.cols-2}
::::{style="font-size:0.65em;"}
**Supported formats:**

| Format | Read | Write |
|:-------|:----:|:-----:|
| VTK legacy `.vtk` | ✅ | ✅ |
| VTK XML `.vtp` | ✅ | ✅ |
| medInria `.fds` | ✅ | ✅ |
| MRtrix `.tck/.tsf` | ✅ | — |
| TrackVis `.trk` | ✅ | — |
| DIPY `.dpy` | ✅ | — |

**Returns** `fiber::streamline` or `fiber::bundle` objects.

<div class="callout-box pend">
⏳ <strong>Not yet on CRAN</strong> — awaiting <code>rvtk</code> (v0.2.0) approval.
</div>
::::

:::: {style="font-size:0.65em; padding-left:1rem;"}
**`rvtk`** — VTK in R (not part of tractoverse)

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
%%| eval: true
%%{init:{"theme":"dark"}}%%
flowchart LR
  R["R package<br/>(riot)"] --> rvtk["rvtk<br/>(infrastructure)"]
  rvtk --> VTK["VTK C++ library<br/>(auto-downloaded)"]

  style R fill:#1c3d5a,stroke:#58a6ff,color:#e6edf3
  style rvtk fill:#2d1f52,stroke:#a371f7,color:#e6edf3
  style VTK fill:#1a3a2a,stroke:#3fb950,color:#e6edf3
```

- Searches for installed VTK or **downloads prebuilt static libs**
- No system dependency for end users
- `use_rvtk()` sets up any downstream package in one call
- Under CRAN submission as `rvtk` v0.2.0

🔗 [astamm.github.io/rvtk](https://astamm.github.io/rvtk/)
::::

:::

---

## `riot` — reading any format

```r
library(riot)

# VTK (all three sub-formats share the same read_bundle() API)
uf_vtk  <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk",  package = "riot"))
uf_vtp  <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtp",  package = "riot"))
uf_fds  <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.fds",  package = "riot"))

# MRtrix and TrackVis
af_tck  <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "AF_left.tck",  package = "riot"))
cc_trk  <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "CCMid.trk",    package = "riot"))

af_tck
#> <bundle [5000 streamlines | 8–54 pts/streamline]>

# Attach metadata at read time
uf_meta <- read_bundle(
  system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk", package = "riot"),
  bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-01", hemisphere = "left")
)
uf_meta@bundle_data
#> $subject   [1] "sub-01"
#> $hemisphere [1] "left"
```

:::{.callout-box style="margin-top:1rem; font-size:0.7em;"}
📦 Install: `pak::pak("tractoverse/riot")` &nbsp;|&nbsp;
🔗 [tractoverse.github.io/riot](https://tractoverse.github.io/riot/)
:::

---

## `rtists` — 3-D visualization {.section-header}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Interactive 3-D** tractography rendering — like `ggplot2` is to the tidyverse.
::::

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
Built on top of [**plotly**](https://plotly.com/r/) → pan, rotate, zoom in any browser.

**`plot3d()`** is an S7 generic with methods for both `streamline` and `bundle`:

| `color =` value | Effect |
|:----------------|:-------|
| `"orientation"` (default) | RGB from fiber direction |
| `@point_data` key | Continuous scale per point |
| `@streamline_data` key | Uniform color per tract |
| CSS string `"steelblue"` | Fixed color |

<div class="callout-box">
🔜 Not yet on CRAN — Submitted
</div>

::::

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

:::: {style="text-align:center; font-size:0.7em;"}

![Orientation coloring](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tractoverse/rtists/master/man/figures/README-orientation-1.png){width=88%}

<span class="dim">Standard DTI color convention: R/G/B = left–right / ant–post / sup–inf</span>

::::

:::

:::

---

## `rtists` — coloring by diffusion metrics

::: {.columns}

::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}

:::: {.column width="49%"}

```r
# Color by FA (per-point scalar)
rtists::plot3d(bundle, color = "FA", palette = "Viridis")
```

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tractoverse/rtists/master/man/figures/README-point-fa-1.png){width=85% fig-align="center"}

::::

:::: {.column width="2%"}
::::

:::: {.column width="49%"}

```r
# Uniform color per streamline (mean FA)
rtists::plot3d(bundle, color = "mean_FA", palette = "RdYlBu")
```

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tractoverse/rtists/master/man/figures/README-streamline-fa-1.png){width=85% fig-align="center"}

::::

:::

:::

::: {.callout-box style="font-size:0.7em;"}
📦 Install: `pak::pak("tractoverse/rtists")` &nbsp;|&nbsp;
🔗 [tractoverse.github.io/rtists](https://tractoverse.github.io/rtists/)
:::

---

## `mascot` — reference datasets {.section-header}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Macroscale Structural Connectomes via Tractography** *(data package)*
::::

::: {.cols-2}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
On-demand download, locally cached.  
All datasets returned as `fiber::bundle` objects.

**HCP1065 population atlas** *(CC BY-SA 4.0)*

- 87 white matter bundles
- Averaged across 1,065 healthy HCP adults
- Yeh et al. (2022), Nature Communications

**TractSeg per-subject bundles** *(CC BY-NC 4.0)*

- 72 tracts × 105 HCP Young Adult subjects
- Segmented with [TractSeg](https://github.com/MIC-DKFZ/TractSeg)
- Wasserthal et al. (2018), NeuroImage
::::

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
```r
library(mascot)

# Browse available bundles
available_bundles("HCP1065")
#>  [1] "Anterior Commissure" "Left Arcuate Fasciculus"   ...

# Load by name (downloads once, cached)
cst_left <- HCP1065_CST_L()
cst_left
#> ── Object of class `fiber::bundle()` with 5577 streamliness
#> and [234–291] points per streamline. ──
#>
#> • Point attributes: none
#> • Streamline attributes: none
#> • Bundle attributes: none

# Or the generic importer
bundle <- import_bundle(
  dataset = "TractSeg", 
  bundle = "Left Arcuate Fasciculus", 
  subjects = "599469"
)
```
::::
:::

---

## `mascot` — real tractography in a line

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
library(mascot)
library(rtists)
# Population-average corticospinal tract (left hemisphere)
cst <- HCP1065_CST_L()
# Instantly visualize
plot3d(cst) # orientation RGB
```

::: {.callout-box style="font-size:0.7em;"}
Not yet on CRAN — submitted &nbsp;|&nbsp;
📦 Install: `pak::pak("tractoverse/mascot")` &nbsp;|&nbsp;
🔗 [tractoverse.github.io/mascot](https://tractoverse.github.io/mascot/)
:::

---

## `midi` — microstructure simulation {.section-header}

::::{style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Microstructure Information from Diffusion Imaging.** Simulate the MR signal attenuation from parametric tissue models — used to validate tractography algorithms and design experiments.
::::

:::{.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}
::::{style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Taxonomy of compartments:**

- Restricted (cylinders, spheres): model axons
- Hindered (tensors): model extra-axonal space
- Free (isotropic): model CSF

**Each compartment** is an R6 object with method `$get_signal()`.
::::
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
::::{style="font-size:0.7em;"}
```r
# ------- Define a cylinder compartment (axon model) -------
restr <- VanGelderenCompartment$new(
  radius      = 0.5,  # µm
  diffusivity = 3     # µm²/ms
)
cyl <- CylinderCompartment$new(
  axis                   = c(0, 0, 1),
  restricted_compartment = restr
)
# ------- Query parameters -------
cyl$get_parameters()
#> $CylinderRadius      [1] 0.5
#> $CylinderDiffusivity [1] 3
# ------- Compute signal attenuation -------
cyl$get_signal(
  small_delta = 30, big_delta = 30,
  G = 0.04, direction = c(0, 0, 1)
)
#> [1] 0.002063
```
::::
:::
:::

::: {.callout-box .ok style="font-size:0.7em;"}
✅ On CRAN &nbsp;|&nbsp;
📦 Install: `install.packages("midi")` &nbsp;|&nbsp;
🔗 [tractoverse.github.io/midi](https://tractoverse.github.io/midi/)
:::

---

## `midi` — simulate & explore

:::{.cols-2}
::::{style="font-size:0.7em;"}
```r
# Simulate a voxel cross-section
out <- simulate_bundle(
  density    = 0.7,
  voxel_size = 5   # µm
)
plot(out)
```

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tractoverse/midi/main/man/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=90%}
::::

::::{style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Interactive Shiny app**

```r
midi::run_app()
```

Or access directly at:  
[midi-pastrami.apps.math.cnrs.fr](https://midi-pastrami.apps.math.cnrs.fr/)

<div class="callout-box" style="margin-top:1rem;">
Interactively explore:<br>
• Signal attenuation curves for each model<br>
• Sensitivity to microstructural parameters<br>
• Optimal experiment design (gradient strength, timings)
</div>
::::
:::

---

## Thank you! {background-color="#0d1117"}

::: {.cols-2}

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**The tractoverse** — tractography analysis in R

<div style="margin: 1.2rem 0;">
  <span class="pkg-badge cran">fiber</span>
  <span class="pkg-badge pend">riot</span>
  <span class="pkg-badge gh">rtists</span>
  <span class="pkg-badge gh">mascot</span>
  <span class="pkg-badge cran">midi</span>
</div>

🔗 [github.com/tractoverse](https://github.com/tractoverse)
::::

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em;"}
**Related infrastructure**

<div style="margin: 1.2rem 0;">
<span class="pkg-badge pend">rvtk</span> — VTK for R (v0.2.0 submitted)  
</div>

🔗  [astamm.github.io/rvtk](https://astamm.github.io/rvtk/)
::::

:::

:::: {style="font-size:0.7em; padding-top:1rem;"}
**JSS paper** — in preparation
::::

::::{.callout-box style="font-size:0.7em;"}
🙏 Partly funded by the French Agence Nationale de la Recherche (project ANR-23-CE45-0037).
::::

::::{style="font-size:0.7em; padding-left:1rem;"}
```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
%%| eval: true
%%{init:{"theme":"dark","themeVariables":{"fontSize":"14px"}}}%%
flowchart TD
  rvtk["rvtk<br/>🔧 VTK for R"]:::infra
  
  fiber["fiber<br/>📦 Data structures"]:::core
  riot["riot<br/>📥 I/O"]:::io
  rtists["rtists<br/>🎨 Visualization"]:::viz
  mascot["mascot<br/>🗂 Datasets"]:::data
  midi["midi<br/>🔬 Simulation"]:::sim
  bot["bot<br/>📐 Optimal transport<br/>(coming soon)"]:::soon

  rvtk --> riot
  fiber --> riot
  fiber --> rtists
  fiber --> mascot
  fiber --> bot

  classDef infra fill:#2d1f52,stroke:#a371f7,color:#e6edf3
  classDef core fill:#21262d,stroke:#e6edf3,color:#e6edf3
  classDef io fill:#1c3d5a,stroke:#58a6ff,color:#e6edf3
  classDef viz fill:#1a3a2a,stroke:#3fb950,color:#e6edf3
  classDef data fill:#2d3b1f,stroke:#d29922,color:#e6edf3
  classDef sim fill:#1a2d35,stroke:#39c5cf,color:#e6edf3
  classDef soon fill:#21262d,stroke:#f85149,color:#8b949e,stroke-dasharray:4
```
::::
